text:
The primary peace treaty that officially brought World War I to an end, specifically between the Allied Powers and Germany, was the **Treaty of Versailles**. This treaty was signed on **June 28, 1919**, in the **Hall of Mirrors (Galerie des Glaces)** within the **Palace of Versailles**, located in the city of **Versailles, France**.

It is important to note that while the armistice on November 11, 1918, ended the fighting, the Treaty of Versailles was the formal document that established the terms of peace and officially concluded the state of war. Other treaties were also signed with the remaining Central Powers in locations around Paris, but the Treaty of Versailles is the most widely recognized and significant treaty ending the war.

FINAL ANSWER: The Treaty of Versailles, signed on June 28, 1919, in the Hall of Mirrors at the Palace of Versailles, France.
